description: Phase 2 tasks — prove the SDK against CRM end-to-end. Per way-to-implement.md, executed sequentially because the kernel-wiring + CRM port surfaces SDK gaps that earlier tasks must learn from. Three workstreams: K (Kernel adapter — wire C5's pure-function pipeline to real Drizzle/InstallRuntime/eventBus), L (CRM port — wrap existing CRM code in a manifest + slot contributions), M (Phase 2 Gate — verify the contract holds end-to-end).
phaseGate: A fresh tenant signs up, lands on Home with default apps + connectors, opens Apps, clicks Install on CRM, approves the permission prompt — and within 30 seconds: schema migrations run, 5 CRM agents seed, Pipeline/Deals/Contacts/Companies appear in the sidebar, default sales pipeline + 7 stages are installed, copilot answers 'what's in my pipeline?' using the new agentDocs. End-to-end with NO SDK code modifications during the port.
phaseRisk: If the port forces an SDK change, pause CRM port immediately and fix the SDK. The port is the contract validation; bending the SDK to fit is failing the validation, not passing it.

tasks.name: Drizzle-backed InstallPipelineDb adapter
tasks.goal: Concrete db ops backed by tenant_apps (C1), wrapped in a Drizzle transaction that the install pipeline can use for atomic multi-step writes.
acceptance: createDrizzleInstallDb(db) returns an InstallPipelineDb
insertTenantApp / deleteTenantApp / getTenantApp all hit the real tenant_apps table
The factory exposes a transaction() helper so the install pipeline can wrap the full sequence inside a Drizzle txn
Integration test against embedded Postgres: install + uninstall end-to-end leaves the row count consistent
doNot: Modify @boringos/control-plane/src/install.ts itself — the adapter is additive
Add Drizzle as a dependency of @boringos/control-plane root if it can stay in this adapter file only

tasks.name: Schema migration runner
tasks.goal: Read manifest.schema/*.sql files from the app's bundle and execute the DDL inside the install transaction.
acceptance: runAppMigrations(tx, app, migrationsDir) applies SQL files in lexicographic order
Already-applied migrations (tracked in a per-app migrations table or file-naming convention) are not re-run
Inside the install transaction: a failed migration rolls back the whole install
Test: a fixture migration creates a namespaced table; install creates it, uninstall (hard) drops it
doNot: Build a generic ORM-agnostic migration framework — Drizzle-specific is fine for v1
Add cross-app migration ordering — apps own their own migration sequence

tasks.name: Agent registration runner
tasks.goal: Read AppDefinition.agents from the bundle and write each into the agents table inside the install txn.
acceptance: registerAppAgents(tx, tenantId, app, definition) creates one agent per AppDefinition.agents[] entry
Agent.persona / runtime / instructions are populated from the definition
Re-installing the same app idempotently replaces prior agents (delete-by-app-id then re-insert)
Test: stub app with 2 agents installs, inspect agents table for both rows
doNot: Wake the agents — that is a per-trigger concern, not part of install
Implement the full agent runtime — only the registration row write

tasks.name: Workflow template registration runner
tasks.goal: Read AppDefinition.workflows and create workflow rows + their cron/event triggers via @boringos/workflow's existing store.
acceptance: registerAppWorkflows(tx, tenantId, app, definition) creates one workflow row per WorkflowTemplate
Triggers (cron / event / webhook) are wired so the framework's existing dispatcher picks them up
Re-install replaces (delete + insert by app id)
Test: install generic-triage's stub manifest, verify the triage-on-inbox workflow exists with the inbox.item_created trigger
doNot: Reimplement the workflow engine — call into @boringos/workflow's store
Run the workflow at install time

tasks.name: Route mounting registrar
tasks.goal: Read AppDefinition.routes and mount the app's Hono router under /api/{appId}, with the app's agentDocs() picked up by the framework's api-catalog context provider.
acceptance: registerAppRoutes(coreApp, app, definition) mounts at /api/{appId}
agentDocs (if exported on the registrar) is registered with the framework's api-catalog provider
Auth middleware wraps the routes (createAuthMiddleware from @boringos/core)
Uninstall unmounts cleanly; subsequent requests to /api/{appId}/* return 404
doNot: Mount routes outside /api/{appId} — apps must stay namespaced

tasks.name: onTenantCreated invoker + ActionContext factory
tasks.goal: After all install steps succeed, invoke the app's onTenantCreated lifecycle hook with a real LifecycleContext (db, log, tenantId). Same shape used to construct ActionContext for command/copilot tool invocations.
acceptance: createLifecycleContext(tx, tenantId) returns a LifecycleContext satisfying the SDK type
createActionContext(tenantId, userId, role) returns an ActionContext usable by command/copilot tool handlers
Failures inside onTenantCreated propagate and trigger install rollback
Test: stub onTenantCreated that throws — install rolls back the agents/workflows/routes inserted by K3-K5
doNot: Spawn agents from onTenantCreated automatically — apps decide what to seed

tasks.name: End-to-end kernel install context factory
tasks.goal: Compose K1-K6 into a single createKernelInstallContext(coreApp, db) function that returns the InstallContext (db + slotRuntime + events) the C5 install pipeline expects.
acceptance: createKernelInstallContext returns a context that wires Drizzle, schema migrator, agent registrar, workflow registrar, route registrar, lifecycle invoker into one InstallContext
End-to-end integration test: install a stub manifest+bundle through C5's installApp() — verify all 6 effects (row, schema, agents, workflows, routes, onTenantCreated) happened
Rollback test: arrange any one step to fail, verify all prior steps undone
Slot runtime is the singleton InstallRuntime from A6
doNot: Add new public types to @boringos/control-plane root — this is internal wiring

tasks.name: Catalog loader from disk for default apps
tasks.goal: Populate DEFAULT_APPS_CATALOG by reading apps/*/boringos.json + the compiled bundle text at boot.
acceptance: loadCatalogFromDisk(appsDir) returns DefaultAppEntry[] for every directory with a boringos.json
Bundle text loaded from manifest.entry path
Manifest hash precomputed via SHA-256
Throws on a malformed manifest (caller decides whether to skip or fail boot)
doNot: Auto-include @boringos/connector-* — they're connectors, not default apps
Bake the apps/ path — accept it as an argument so deployments can configure

tasks.name: Wire installDefaultApps into framework's onTenantCreated hook
tasks.goal: Fresh signup with tenantName auto-installs every catalog entry through the regular install pipeline.
acceptance: After a successful signup with tenantName, generic-triage and generic-replier are installed with active status
Failure to install one default app does not block signup; an activity-log entry records the failure
Existing tenants don't double-install on framework restart
Test: signup, query /api/admin/apps?tenantId=<id>, expect 2 entries
doNot: Mark default apps undeletable — they follow the regular lifecycle

tasks.name: HTTP install endpoint + Apps screen wire-up
tasks.goal: POST /api/admin/apps/install accepts a manifest URL, fetches + validates + installs, returns the install record. Shell's Apps screen Install button calls this.
acceptance: POST /api/admin/apps/install with { url } returns 200 + InstallRecord on success, 4xx with structured errors on validation failure
Shell's Browse tab Install button triggers fetch+validate then shows the PermissionPrompt; Approve calls the install endpoint
Install from URL tab does the same with a user-provided URL (ungated by marketplace)
Permission denied (non-admin user) returns 403
doNot: Allow individual capability dropping in the prompt — all-or-nothing in v1

tasks.name: HTTP uninstall endpoint + Apps screen Installed tab wire-up
tasks.goal: DELETE /api/admin/apps/:appId?mode=soft|hard executes the C6 uninstall pipeline. Shell's Installed tab Uninstall button calls this with cascade-warning UX.
acceptance: DELETE /api/admin/apps/:appId?mode=soft returns 200 + UninstallResult; cascade is reported in the body when force=false
Installed tab Uninstall button: shows cascade warning if non-empty; user clicks Continue → re-calls with force=true
Hard mode requires a separate confirm dialog because it is irreversible
doNot: Auto-uninstall dependent apps — only warn

tasks.name: Add boringos.json at the CRM repo root
tasks.goal: CRM gets a manifest declaring its identity, capabilities, entity types, and entry path. Validates against the @boringos/app-sdk schema.
acceptance: kind: app, id: crm, version matches CRM's package version
entityTypes declares crm_contact, crm_company, crm_deal (shareable: true), crm_pipeline, crm_pipeline_stage, crm_activity

capabilities cover everything CRM does today: entities.own:write, entities.core:write, agents:register, workflows:register, contextProviders:register, routes:register, slots:nav, slots:dashboard.widget, slots:entity.detail, slots:entity.action, slots:settings.panel, slots:copilot.tool, connectors:use:google, connectors:use:slack, memory:read, memory:write
validateManifest passes; isAppManifest narrows correctly
skills field references CRM's existing agent skill markdown files
doNot: Touch CRM server code — manifest is purely declarative
Add capabilities CRM doesn't actually use — capability honesty matters

tasks.name: Export defineApp() from CRM server
tasks.goal: CRM's existing server code (schema DDL, agents, workflows, routes, contextProviders, onTenantCreated) gets wrapped in a single defineApp({...}) export.
acceptance: @boringos-crm/server exports a default defineApp({...}) bundling: schema (path), 5 agents (Email Triage, Contact Enrichment, Company Enrichment, Deal Analyst, Follow-up Writer), 2 workflow templates (Email Ingest, Calendar Check), context providers (createCompanyProfileProvider et al), routes registrar with agentDocs
onTenantCreated wraps the existing pipeline-seed logic from tenant.ts
The standalone server entry still works (boots on port 3001) — this is additive
TypeScript: AppDefinition shape is satisfied
doNot: Rewrite the existing server logic — wrap it
Strip the standalone server yet — that is L11

tasks.name: Validate CRM manifest end-to-end against the install pipeline
tasks.goal: Run @boringos/control-plane's validateManifestFull against the CRM manifest + bundle. Surface any honesty warnings.
acceptance: Test loads CRM's manifest + compiled bundle text and runs validateManifestFull
Schema validation passes with zero errors
Capability honesty: warnings are acceptable in v1; document any unexpected ones
If schema fails, the test exits with the Phase 1 SDK gap that needs fixing — STOP and fix before proceeding
doNot: Suppress warnings to make the test pass — they are actionable

tasks.name: First slot port — Pipeline page as slots.pages contribution
tasks.goal: The single most important UI port. Lift CRM's PipelinePage into a UIDefinition slot the shell renders inside its chrome.
acceptance: @boringos-crm/web exports defineUI({ pages: { pipeline: { id: 'pipeline', component: PipelinePage } } })
After installing CRM, the shell sidebar shows a 'Pipeline' nav entry; clicking it navigates to /crm/pipeline; PipelinePage renders inside the shell's Layout
Pipeline page still calls /api/crm/* routes for data; the shell's BoringOSClient passes auth headers transparently
Visual: pipeline kanban renders with all stages + deals
doNot: Rewrite the kanban — only repackage as a slot
Port the other CRM screens yet — vertical slice, one at a time

tasks.name: Slot port — Deals + DealDetail
tasks.goal: DealsPage as a nav slot; DealDetailPage as the shell's entity detail route for crm_deal.
acceptance: Deals appears in sidebar under the 'Apps' group
Clicking a deal opens the shell's entity detail surface; DealDetail's tabs render via the shell's chrome
Existing data flows (TanStack Query against /api/crm/deals/*) keep working
doNot: Touch DealForm, EntityActions, ActivityTimeline components — those move with their parents in later tasks

tasks.name: Slot port — Contacts + ContactDetail
tasks.goal: Same pattern as L5 for the Contact entity.
acceptance: Contacts in the sidebar; clicking opens contact detail with the Dossier view intact
DossierView renders as an entity.detail panel
doNot: Re-export ContactForm into the shell — modal forms are still local to CRM

tasks.name: Slot port — Companies + CompanyDetail
tasks.goal: Same pattern for the Company entity.
acceptance: Companies in the sidebar; CompanyDetail with its dossier renders cleanly
doNot: Add cross-app entity reads yet — that's Phase 3 with Accounts

tasks.name: EntityActions for crm_deal
tasks.goal: Lift CRM's EntityActions component into ui.entityActions slot contributions on crm_deal records.
acceptance: Deal detail page's action bar shows: 'Send follow-up' (wakes Follow-up Writer agent), 'Run analyst' (wakes Deal Analyst), 'Mark won/lost', etc.
Each action is a typed EntityAction<crm_deal> with an invoke handler
Handlers receive ActionContext from the kernel adapter (K6) — emit events, write activity log, etc.
doNot: Add cross-app actions (e.g. 'Send invoice' from Accounts) — that lands when Accounts ships in Phase 3

tasks.name: Settings panel — Pipeline configuration
tasks.goal: CRM contributes a settings.panel for pipeline + stage configuration. Shell's Settings screen shows it as a tab.
acceptance: After CRM install, Settings sidebar shows a 'Pipeline configuration' tab under the per-app section
Panel allows adding/removing stages, reordering, setting probabilities
Saves go to /api/crm/pipelines via the existing routes
doNot: Build a full design-tokens CRM customization — that's the BrandProvider's job, not CRM's

tasks.name: Email Triage agent split — CRM Email Lens
tasks.goal: The CRM-aware part of the existing email-triage agent moves into a new agent that subscribes to triage.classified (emitted by generic-triage) and reads metadata.triage instead of re-classifying.
acceptance: New agent crm.email-lens subscribes to triage.classified
Reads inbox item's metadata.triage; never re-classifies
Adds CRM-specific interpretation: links to a Contact, links to an active Deal, drafts a CRM-aware reply that references the deal stage
Old crm.email-triage instructions migrated where useful into email-lens; redundant general-classification logic dropped
End-to-end test: synthesize an inbox.item_created event, observe generic-triage classify, then crm.email-lens layer on top
doNot: Re-classify in the lens — that's generic-triage's job
Auto-archive — out of scope for v1

tasks.name: Strip CRM standalone server bits
tasks.goal: Once CRM is fully shell-hosted, the standalone @boringos-crm/server entry that boots on :3001 is no longer needed. Delete the entry script; keep the routes/agents/etc as library exports the kernel adapter consumes.
touch: ../boringos-crm/packages/server/package.json (drop the dev script that booted port 3001)
acceptance: @boringos-crm/server exports defineApp + library symbols; no longer ships a standalone HTTP server
boringos-crm/packages/web is similarly trimmed (already partially done in A8); the port-5173 dev script is removed
CRM still typechecks; CRM's published packages still build
doNot: Delete CRM domain code (routes, agents, schema) — those are still used as a library

tasks.name: Fix the stale @boringos/workflow-ui link in CRM
tasks.goal: Resolve the pre-existing CRM typecheck regression from the framework rename (boringos → boringos-framework). Workflow-detail/editor/run pages depend on @boringos/workflow-ui at a stale path.
acceptance: boringos-crm/packages/web typecheck reports zero @boringos/workflow-ui errors (was 5)
WorkflowDetail / WorkflowEditor / WorkflowRunDetail pages still build
These pages remain in CRM as the canonical workflow editor surface (out of scope for shell port — too heavy)
doNot: Move workflow-ui into the shell — the editor stays a CRM-side dependency

tasks.name: End-to-end install timing benchmark
tasks.goal: Measure the full Apps → Install CRM → ready latency. Phase 2 Gate target: under 30 seconds.
acceptance: Test boots a fresh tenant, calls POST /api/admin/apps/install with the local CRM manifest URL, polls until tenant_apps.status = 'active', records elapsed ms
Asserts elapsed < 30,000ms
Records median + p95 across 3 runs to a phase2-gate-results.md report
doNot: Run this against npm-published CRM — uses local file: protocol or a test fixture

tasks.name: Slot contributions visible after install
tasks.goal: Verify the shell's sidebar populates with CRM's nav entries within one render after install.
acceptance: After install, slotRegistry.list('pages') contains entries for crm.pipeline, crm.deals, crm.contacts, crm.companies
slotRegistry.list('entityActions', { appId: 'crm' }) contains the deal actions
slotRegistry.list('settingsPanels') contains the pipeline-config panel
doNot: Rely on a full DOM render harness — registry-level assertions are sufficient for Phase 2 Gate

tasks.name: Agent + workflow seeding verification
tasks.goal: After CRM install, all 5 expected CRM agents and 2 workflow templates are present in the framework tables.
acceptance: After install, agents table contains 5 CRM agents (the new email-lens replacing the old email-triage, plus contact-enrichment, company-enrichment, deal-analyst, follow-up-writer)
workflows table contains the email-ingest and calendar-check templates
Default sales pipeline + 7 stages exist (from CRM's onTenantCreated)
doNot: Test agent execution — only registration

tasks.name: Copilot picks up CRM context (agentDocs)
tasks.goal: After install, the framework's api-catalog context provider injects CRM's agentDocs into copilot prompts.
acceptance: Build the api-catalog context for copilot in a tenant with CRM installed
The catalog markdown contains CRM's route descriptions (contacts, deals, etc.)
Without CRM installed, the same catalog has no CRM section
doNot: Spawn a real copilot session — context-provider output is enough

tasks.name: SDK regression check — was Phase 1 SDK code touched?
tasks.goal: Audit the diff between Phase 1's tip and Phase 2's tip. If any file under packages/@boringos/app-sdk/ changed during the port, that's evidence the SDK contract was wrong; document why.
readFirst: docs/phases/phase-2.md (the 'no SDK code modifications during the port' clause)
acceptance: Diff shows zero changes under packages/@boringos/app-sdk/ between Phase 1 close (commit f7e78ac at the time of Phase 2 start) and Phase 2 close
If non-zero: each change is documented with the gap it filled and the test that surfaced it
Either way, M5 outcome is appended to docs/tests/phase2-gate-results.md as the public record
doNot: Pretend SDK changes didn't happen — honesty about the contract validation outcome IS the Phase 2 deliverable
